Home1: The production of chemicals is heavily dependent on fossil fuels and accounts for 5~6% of global greenhouse gas emissions
Home2: The U.S. produces over $5 bn worth of biogas (a mix of CH4 and CO2) a year from landfills, farms, and wastewater treatment plants...
Home3: ...but 60% is either wasted or used in low-margin applications like electricity or CHP
Home4: repurposes biogas into dimethyl ether (DME), an in-demand and versatile chemical
Home5: DME’s global market size is over $10 bn and is expected to double over the next decade
Home6: Selling us your biogas instead of flaring or generating grid electricity can unlock a new revenue stream
Home7: Aerosol companies call DME the "Rolls Royce of propellants," perfect for products like air fresheners, hairspray, and more
Home8: Similar to ethanol and gasoline, DME can be blended with propane to create a low-carbon and more efficient fuel
Home9: Every kg of Rise's DME avoids nearly 3 kg CO2eq
Home10: That's almost 5 miles in a Ford F-150 avoided per kg of Rise's DME
Tech3: Syngas is the building block for many other chemicals such as methanol, DMC, SAF, and formaldehyde
Tech4: CLEAN and UPGRADE are fully commercialized. Our novel REFORMing step enables the competitive production of chemicals from biogas
Tech6: Allows for rapid and in-situ deployment, greater reach, lower CAPEX, quicker payback, and easy scalability
Tech7: Rise's DME is produced without the extraction, processing, and transportation of fossil fuels as raw materials
Tech8: Rise's DME competes pricewise with traditional fossil-fuel derived DME
